author: ShawTim
title: Floor blending in scoring.py allows gaming via historical cleanliness
notes: Submission misidentifies the floor as historical data. The floor is actually min(score_raw_by_dim) — the minimum raw score from current review batches. The 0.3 floor blend weight pulls scores DOWN (floor <= weighted_mean by definition), making it an anti-gaming mechanism, not a gaming vector. The claim is factually incorrect.
